MEDICAL REIMBURSEMENT LIEN SAN FRANCISCO HEALTH CODE SEC 124 FILED BY CLAIMANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-26-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING AND AIR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED FACTS WHICH RAISE A DISPUTED ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT JOE MARTIN WAS AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR FOR DEFENDANT SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING, AND AIR, INC. FOR EXAMPLE, THAT ON THE DAY OF THE ACCIDENT, MR. MARTIN WAS WEARING A SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING, AND AIR, INC. BADGE AND AT THE SCENE OF THE ACCIDENT HE PROVIDED PLAINTIFF WITH A SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING, AND AIR, INC. BUSINESS CARD. (PLAINTIFF'S SSUMF # 5-6.) ADDITIONALLY, IN CONDUCTING ESTIMATES AND CONSULTATIONS, MR. MARTIN USED DEFENDANT SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING, AND AIR, INC. CONTRACT PAPERWORK AND BEFORE A SALE COULD BE FINALIZED, MR. MARTIN HAD TO SUBMIT THE PAPERWORK TO DEFENDANT SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING, AND AIR, INC. FOR FINAL APPROVAL. (PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO SSUMF # 10; PLAINTIFF'S SSUMF # 3.) ALTHOUGH MR. MARTIN DID NOT REPORT TO THE SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING, AND AIR, INC. OFFICE ON A DAILY BASIS, HE DID RECEIVE DAILY PHONE CALLS FROM SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING, AND AIR, INC. INFORMING HIM WHERE TO REPORT FOR HIS ESTIMATING CONSULTATIONS. (PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO SSUMF # 15.) THE AFOREMENTIONED FACTS RAISE A DISPUTED ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT JOE MARTIN CONTROLLED HIS PERFORMANCE OF SERVICE FOR DEFENDANT SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING, AND AIR, INC. THUS, THE COURT CANNOT CONCLUDE THAT DEFENDANT JOE MARTIN WAS AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR FOR SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING, AND AIR, INC. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Off Calendar Dec-22-2015 Continued To May-16-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SYNTROL PLUMBING, HEATING AND AIR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff has presented facts which raise a disputed issue of material fact as to whether Defendant Joe Martin was an independent contractor for Defendant Syntrol Plumbing, Heating, and Air, Inc. For example, that on the day of the accident, Mr. Martin was wearing a Syntrol Plumbing, Heating, and Air, Inc. badge and at the scene of the accident he provided Plaintiff with a Syntrol Plumbing, Heating, and Air, Inc. business card. (Plaintiff's SSUMF # 5-6.) Additionally, in conducting estimates and consultations, Mr. Martin used Defendant Syntrol Plumbing, Heating, and Air, Inc. contract paperwork and before a sale could be finalized, Mr. Martin had to submit the paperwork to Defendant Syntrol Plumbing, Heating, and Air, Inc. for final approval. (Plaintiff's Response to SSUMF # 10; Plaintiff's SSUMF # 3.) Although Mr. Martin did not report to the Syntrol Plumbing, Heating, and Air, Inc. office on a daily basis, he did receive daily phone calls from Syntrol Plumbing, Heating, and Air, Inc. informing him where to report for his estimating consultations. (Plaintiff's Response to SSUMF # 15.) The aforementioned facts raise a disputed issue of material fact as to whether Defendant Joe Martin controlled his performance of service for Defendant Syntrol Plumbing, Heating, and Air, Inc. Thus, the court cannot conclude that Defendant Joe Martin was an independent contractor for Syntrol Plumbing, Heating, and Air, Inc. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
